text: NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC personnel to create a new LNP NPA-NXX for a Service Provider.
NPAC SMS shall only send updates for subscription versions that are not filtered on the Local SMS for a mass update request.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC personnel to select Subscription Versions for mass update based on user defined criteria.
NPAC SMS shall require at least one selection criteria for a mass update.
NPAC SMS shall create an old Subscription Version with a new version id for an active Subscription Version involved in a mass update.
NPAC SMS shall broadcast no data to Local SMSs due to the creation of an old Subscription Version during a mass update.
NPAC SMS shall log an exception and proceed with Mass Update processing upon finding a subscription version in sending status.
NPAC SMS shall produce an exception report for NPAC Personnel listing Subscription Versions not processed during Mass Update.
NPAC SMS shall require NPAC personnel to specify a Service Provider ID when entering Selection Criteria for a Mass Update.
NPAC SMS shall identify all Subscription Versions affected by mass changes and carry out updates to Local SMSs.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC Personnel to update block holder default routing information via mass update.
NPAC SMS shall reject a mass update request if the TN Range intersects an existing 1K Block.
NPAC SMS shall reject a mass update request to a Block if the status is NOT active.
NPAC SMS shall download Number Pooling Block Information for mass updates when the Service Provider EDR Indicator is TRUE.
NPAC SMS shall download Number Pooling Block Information using Subscription Versions when the Service Provider EDR Indicator is FALSE.
NPAC SMS shall create an old Block with a new version id for an active Block involved in a mass update.
NPAC SMS shall support an off-line batch download to update Local SMSs with network and subscription data.
NPAC SMS shall communicate creation or deletion of NPA-NXX and LRN data to Local SMSs and SOAs.
NPAC SMS shall inform all Service Providers about the availability of NPA-NXXs for porting via specified interfaces.
NPAC SMS shall post new Service Providers and LRNs on the Web bulletin board.
NPAC SMS shall allow Service Providers to add NPA-NXX data via specified interfaces.
NPAC SMS shall allow Service Providers to delete NPA-NXX data via specified interfaces.
NPAC SMS shall provide a mechanism for Service Providers to indicate download preferences for NPA-NXX and LRN data.
NPAC SMS shall periodically perform database extracts of active Subscription Versions to an ASCII file.
NPAC SMS shall store database extract files at the NPAC SMS FTP site.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC personnel to specify an NPA-NXX for database extract file creation.
NPAC SMS shall prevent the entry of pipe characters as part of text strings.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC personnel to create a new LRN for a service provider.
NPAC SMS shall synchronize its system clock using NTP to a Stratum 1 host.
NPAC SMS shall support a permissive dialing period during NPA splits.
NPAC SMS shall verify that new and old NPA-NXXs involved in an NPA Split exist upon data entry.
NPAC SMS shall prevent an NPA-NXX involved in an NPA split from being deleted during permissive dialing.
NPAC SMS shall retain the Subscription Version Id of Versions involved in an NPA Split.
NPAC SMS shall update all non-archived Subscription Versions with the new NPA at the start of permissive dialing.
NPAC SMS shall automatically delete the old NPA-NXX upon reaching the end of the permissive dialing period.
NPAC SMS shall automatically create a new NPA-NXX-X when a valid request is made to add an NPA Split.
NPAC SMS shall reject an NPA-NXX-X addition if the NPA-NXX is scheduled for an NPA Split.
NPAC SMS shall support definition of long and short business hours for business time calculation.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC operations personnel to add or delete business holidays.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC personnel to store a primary service provider id with an associated service provider id.
NPAC SMS shall reject any SOA association attempt by a Service Provider Id associated with a primary ID.
NPAC SMS shall support recovery of notifications for associated Service Providers via the primary association.
NPAC SMS shall provide a mechanism to indicate if a Service Provider supports receiving NPA-NXX-X data.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC Personnel to manage NPA-NXX-X Holder information via the Administrative Interface.
NPAC SMS shall validate that the effective date of an NPA-NXX-X is not before the NPA-NXX effective date.
NPAC SMS shall apply NPA-NXX Filters to NPA-NXX-X downloads.
NPAC SMS shall require NPAC personnel to specify NPA-NXX-X Holder SPID and Effective Date during creation.
NPAC SMS shall reject NPA-NXX-X creation if pending-like no-active Subscription Versions exist for the range.
NPAC SMS shall provide output destination options for reports including print, fax, and e-mail.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC personnel to modify the effective date for an NPA-NXX-X.
NPAC SMS shall reject NPA-NXX-X deletion if the associated Block contains a status other than Active.
NPAC SMS shall verify the Block Holder SPID matches the accessControl SPID for SOA Block Activation.
NPAC SMS shall set the status of a Block to Active if all Local SMSs respond successfully to creation.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC personnel and the SOA interface to create a Subscription Version.
NPAC SMS shall prohibit a Subscription Version in conflict from being activated.
NPAC SMS shall notify SOAs and Local SMSs when an NPA-NXX is first used for a Subscription Version.
NPAC SMS shall verify that the due date is not in the past upon Subscription Version creation.
NPAC SMS shall verify that an input LRN is associated with the new Service Provider.
NPAC SMS shall notify Service Providers when a Subscription Version is set to pending.
NPAC SMS shall allow only NPAC personnel and the current Service Provider to modify their own active Subscription Versions.
NPAC SMS shall send an error message to the SOA if no cause code is specified when setting conflict.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC personnel and SOA/Local SMS users to query Subscription data.
NPAC SMS shall return an error message if no data is found for a query.
NPAC SMS shall reject a message to manually create a Subscription Version with LNP Type POOL.
NPAC SMS shall verify that 1000 individual TNs exist for a Block upon successful creation.
NPAC SMS shall provide a secure NPAC SOA Low-tech Interface.
NPAC SMS shall provide a tunable parameter for the number of message retry attempts.
NPAC SMS shall support recovery of all CMIP notifications emitted over defined interfaces.
NPAC SMS shall recover missed network data downloads in time sequence order.
NPAC SMS shall require unique user identification codes to identify all personnel.
NPAC SMS shall store passwords in a one-way encrypted form.
NPAC SMS shall enforce password aging with a default of 90 days.
NPAC SMS shall display the date and time of the user last successful access upon login.
NPAC SMS shall only allow the NPAC Security Administrator to authorize users.
NPAC SMS shall protect service provider data from access by unauthorized users.
NPAC SMS shall use SNMP to monitor system resources and detect error conditions.
NPAC SMS shall generate a security audit log sufficient for after-the-fact investigation.
NPAC SMS shall maintain security audit data online for a minimum of 90 days.
NPAC SMS shall ensure that no service provider action causes the system to be unavailable to others.
NPAC SMS shall have documented procedures for software and data backup.
NPAC SMS shall support data origin authentication for each incoming message.
NPAC SMS shall support detection of message replay and modification.
NPAC SMS shall use a public key crypto system to provide digital signatures.
NPAC SMS shall ensure that notifications are sent in confirmed mode.
NPAC SMS shall be responsible for ensuring the system clock is accurate to within two minutes of GMT.
NPAC SMS shall record all audit results in an audit log.
NPAC SMS shall allow NPAC personnel to view all audit requests including those from Service Providers.
NPAC SMS shall support on-line file transfer capabilities for report files.
NPAC SMS shall maintain an Error Log to track transaction and transmission errors.
NPAC SMS shall be available 24 hours a day with defined exceptions.
NPAC SMS shall be 99.9 percent reliable.
NPAC SMS shall support a mean time to repair of less than or equal to 1 hour.
NPAC SMS shall support duplexing of major hardware components.
NPAC SMS shall ensure transaction response time is less than 3 seconds for 95% of transactions.
NPAC SMS shall measure and record resource usage on a per Service Provider basis.
